data.title: Roku stock drops before Q4 earnings, analysts expect earnings of 28 cents per share on revenue of $1.35 billion.
data.description: Roku Inc (NASDAQ: ROKU) is experiencing a decline in shares today as investors anticipate the release of its quarterly earnings report after market close. Analysts predict earnings of 28 cents per share on revenue reaching $1.35 billion. The market is curious about management's outlook for the future. In the past, Roku has seen varied reactions to earnings reports, with the stock surging 6% after beating expectations by 8 cents last quarter. The company's stock performance is currently under scrutiny as it retraces from recent highs.

data.title: KBRA gives ratings to GCAT 2026-NQM1 Trust worth $375.7M, has 10 classes, 668 mortgages, credit score avg 751, modest equity.
data.description: KBRA has given preliminary ratings to 10 classes of mortgage pass-through certificates from GCAT 2026-NQM1 Trust. This non-prime RMBS transaction is valued at $375.7 million and is backed by 668 residential mortgages. The loans in the collateral pool have been underwritten using alternative income documentation and borrowers have an average original credit score of 751. Additionally, there is a modest amount of equity in each mortgaged property.

data.title: Baxter's shares fall as 2026 profit guidance disappoints. Q4 EPS at 44 cents, sales reach $2.974 billion, up 8% yoy.
data.description: Baxter International Inc. (NYSE: BAX) is currently trading near the lower end of its 52-week range between $17.4 and $37.74, as per data from Benzinga Pro. In the fourth quarter, Baxter reported an adjusted EPS of 44 cents, falling short of both the management guidance range of 52-57 cents and the Wall Street estimate of 54 cents. This decrease was mainly attributed to an unfavorable product mix. The company achieved sales of $2.974 billion in the quarter, marking an 8% increase year over year on a reported basis and a 3% increase on an operational basis, surpassing the consensus of $2.824 billion. Management had initially anticipated a 2% growth in fourth-quarter sales on a reported basis and a 2% decline on an operational basis. Medical Products & Therapies segment sales accounted for around $1.39 billion, showing a 6% increase on a reported basis and a 4% increase on an operational basis.

data.title: TRUCE® Software receives Series B funding to scale up its AI telematics for fleets and phone management tools for schools.
data.description: In Lisle, Illinois, TRUCE® Software recently completed a Series B funding round aimed at enhancing its mobile-first AI telematics designed for fleets and implementing phone management solutions for K-12 schools. The funding round garnered a significant investment to bolster the company's expansion plans.

data.title: Novo Nordisk plans to launch Wegovy in vials to compete with Eli Lilly in the obesity market. Lilly's vials are one-third of new patients, but Novo's pricing is undisclosed. Novo will first introduce the vials in the U.S. before other markets.
data.description: Novo Nordisk A/S (NVO) is set to introduce its widely-popular obesity drug Wegovy in vial format, as it strategizes to compete against Eli Lilly and Co. (LLY) in the growing weight-loss market. The drug is currently marketed in prefilled injector pens, but the company aims to diversify its offerings. Rival Lilly began offering vials of Zepbound in 2024 due to shortages, with CEO Dave Ricks noting vials now make up a third of new weight-loss therapy patients. Novo Nordisk has not revealed pricing for the vial format and plans to launch in the U.S. first before expanding to other markets. CEO Mike Doustdar leads this move to maintain a strong position in the face of increasing industry competition.

data.title: Grindr's "Who’s the A**hole?" podcast returns for Season 4 with host Katya, promising more queer excellence and humor.
data.description: In West Hollywood, California, Grindr is relaunching its popular podcast "Who's the Asshole?" for a fourth season. Hosted by drag queen and comedian Katya Zamolodchikova, the latest season promises queer excellence and humor. The podcast delves into outrageous scenarios, hot takes, and messy dilemmas with sharp wit and honesty. Grindr, known as the Global Gayborhood in Your Pocket™, is excited to bring back the award-winning show for fans.
